title: Apply: cell parts and functions
description: Apply your understanding of cell parts and functions in this set of free practice questions aligned to NGSS standards.

assessment_items.question_content:
The diagram below shows an animal cell and its parts. 

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which arrow is pointing to the cell part that contains most of the cell’s genes?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

options.alt: A circle represents an animal cell. The cell contains many cell parts with different shapes. An arrow labeled A points to a bean-shaped cell part. An arrow labeled B points to a medium-sized circular cell part that has squiggly lines inside. An arrow labeled C points to the outermost part of the cell, which is shown as an outline of the cell.

choices.content: A
B
C

assessment_items.question_content:
The diagram below shows an animal cell and its parts. 

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which arrow is pointing to a cell part that breaks down sugars to provide energy for the cell?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

options.alt: A circle represents an animal cell. The cell contains many cell parts with different shapes. An arrow labeled A points to a bean-shaped cell part. An arrow labeled B points to a medium-sized circular cell part that has squiggly lines inside. An arrow labeled C points to the outermost part of the cell, which is shown as an outline of the cell.

choices.content: A
B
C

The diagram below shows an animal cell and its parts. 

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which arrow is pointing to the cell part that controls what goes into and out of the cell?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

options.alt: A circle represents an animal cell. The cell contains many cell parts with different shapes. An arrow labeled A points to a bean-shaped cell part. An arrow labeled B points to a medium-sized circular cell part that has squiggly lines inside. An arrow labeled C points to the outermost part of the cell, which is shown as an outline of the cell.

assessment_items.question_content:
The diagram below shows a plant cell and its parts.

[[☃ image 1]]

**In the cell diagram, which arrow is pointing to the nucleus?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

options.alt: A four-sided figure with rounded corners represents a plant cell. The cell contains many cell parts with different shapes. An arrow labeled A points to a small green oval with stacks of darker green ovals inside. An arrow labeled B points to a medium-sized circle that has squiggly lines inside. An arrow labeled C points to the outermost part of the cell, which is shown as a thick outline of the figure.

choices.content: A
B
C

The diagram below shows a plant cell and its parts.

[[☃ image 1]]

**In the cell diagram, which arrow is pointing to the cell wall?**

[[☃ explanation 1]]

options.alt: A four-sided figure with rounded corners represents a plant cell. The cell contains many cell parts with different shapes. An arrow labeled A points to a small green oval with stacks of darker green ovals inside. An arrow labeled B points to a medium-sized circle that has squiggly lines inside. assessment_items.question_content:
The diagram below shows a plant cell and its parts.

[[☃ image 1]]

**In the cell diagram, which arrow is pointing to a chloroplast?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

options.alt: A four-sided figure with rounded corners represents a plant cell. The cell contains many cell parts with different shapes. An arrow labeled A points to a small green oval with stacks of darker green ovals inside. An arrow labeled B points to a medium-sized circle that has squiggly lines inside. An arrow labeled C points to the outermost part of the cell, which is shown as a thick outline of the figure.

choices.content: A
B

assessment_items.question_content:
**What would happen to a plant cell if its chloroplasts stopped working properly?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The cell would become an animal cell.
The cell would not be able to make sugars via photosynthesis.
The cell wall would take over the chloroplasts’ function.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What would happen to an animal cell if its mitochondria stopped working properly?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The cell would not have energy to live and grow.
Genetic information could not be stored inside the cell.
The cell membrane would take over the mitochondria’s function.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What would happen to a plant cell if its cell wall stopped working properly?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The cell would lose its rigid shape.
The cell would not be able to make sugar via photosynthesis.
The chloroplasts would take over the cell wall’s function.

assessment_items.question_content:
**How do mitochondria and chloroplasts work together to help a plant cell stay alive?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Chloroplasts make sugars that mitochondria break down for energy.
Mitochondria store the sugar that chloroplasts need to carry out photosynthesis.
Mitochondria make sugars that chloroplasts can use to provide structure to the plant.

assessment_items.question_content:
**How does the nucleus help the plant cell wall carry out its function?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The nucleus contains genes that provide information for building parts of the cell wall.
The nucleus stores sugars that are used in building the cell wall.
The nucleus breaks down sugar to provide energy that the cell wall uses to build its structure.

assessment_items.question_content:
**How do mitochondria help the cell membrane carry out its function?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Mitochondria contain the genes that are required to build the cell membrane.
Mitochondria make sugars that the cell membrane breaks down to release energy.
Mitochondria provide the energy that the cell membrane needs to move substances into and out of the cell.
